comment:
Hardware probe: prove that a custom ZDL can invoke state[24] (the BPM-to-samples helper) without freezing. Uses a single-byte patch of linesel_handlers.bin that changes the knob1 handler's LDW *+A7[31] (read knob) to LDW *+A7[24] (BPM helper). Everything else is the proven LineSel-cloned shape.

The handler still passes B4=2 to the callback (the LineSel knob_id), which is NOT TAPEECH3's convention for state[24]. So the *value* this probe writes to params[5] is probably garbage. The probe's primary signal is whether the pedal stays alive when the knob is touched.

What to watch for after flashing:
  1. Load — does SyncProbe show up in the Filter category browser?
  2. Unbypass — does the pedal stay alive when activated?
  3. Knob interaction — does turning the knob freeze the pedal?
  4. Tap tempo — if alive, does tapping the tempo button change the audio output?

The audio body amplifies / DC-offsets the input by params[5] so any value that lands there is audible.
gid_comment: 0x02 = Filter category. Stays out of Delay so it does not collide with TapeEcho4.
fxid_comment: 0x019A. New FXID for the sync probe so it does not overlap any existing build.

params.name: Sync
params.flags_comment: 0x28 — the tempo-sync bit. Same value the linker emits for stock Pattern-B SYNC slots (TAPEECH3/STOMPDLY).
params.comment: Single-page slot. The patched handler stores state[24]'s return value (not the knob value) into params[5].
